design_basis: Batch 01 favored a larger proposer plurality and judge-free distributed review; MODAFFINE and GROWBLOCK were the clearest family weaknesses.

strategies.name: Generalist Vote 15
strategies.description: Frozen repeated control: fifteen independent generalists followed by deterministic plurality.

stages.instruction: Solve independently and return the single best complete answer for every case.

strategies.name: Diversified Vote 15
strategies.description: Frozen repeated control: fifteen independently prompted diverse solvers followed by deterministic plurality.

stages.instruction: Solve independently through the assigned lens, but search other allowed families if that lens fails.

strategies.name: E04 Verified Gate
strategies.description: Frozen repeated control: seven proposers, two verifiers, and one judge with a unanimity gate.

stages.instruction: Solve independently and justify one complete allowed mechanism.
Reconstruct the strongest candidates independently and report the answer that survives complete replay.
Choose one exact candidate or a fully checked replacement. Do not average competing tuples.

strategies.name: E04 Proposer Pool
strategies.description: Frozen repeated control: the seven-proposer pool from Experiment 04 with the decision layer removed.

stages.instruction: Solve independently and justify one complete allowed mechanism.

strategies.name: Winning Twelve-Solver Challenge Gate
strategies.description: Direct replication of Batch 01's winning 12-proposer base with a three-challenger conservative gate.

stages.instruction: Solve independently and require a full-prefix mechanism.
Try to disprove the plurality and report a replacement only when fully reconstructed.

strategies.name: Expanded Judge-Free Weighted Review
strategies.description: Nine proposers, three critics, and three verifiers extend Batch 01's strongest true orchestration without a judge.

stages.instruction: Solve independently and return one exact full-prefix answer.
Falsify weak candidates and return the strongest complete answer.
Independently reconstruct and replay the strongest surviving mechanisms.

strategies.name: Tightened Correction Hybrid
strategies.description: The Batch 01 correction hybrid with a stricter three-reviewer support gate to reduce harmful changes.

stages.instruction: Solve independently through the assigned lens.
Falsify weak candidates and identify the strongest complete alternative.
Reconstruct the strongest surviving mechanisms from scratch.
Select one exact answer using complete-prefix evidence.

strategies.name: Repeated Critic-Supported Dual Judge
strategies.description: Replication of the Batch 01 dual-judge system that made one useful and no harmful corrections.

stages.instruction: Solve independently and give the simplest complete mechanism.
Try to falsify the proposals and repair them only with a complete allowed mechanism.
Choose the answer with the strongest complete-prefix support.

strategies.name: Large-Base Judge-Free Review
strategies.description: Ten proposers preserve a strong base while two critics and three verifiers contribute weighted evidence without a judge.

stages.instruction: Solve independently and require a complete mechanism.
Identify concrete failures and the strongest repaired answer.
Reconstruct and replay the leading candidates exactly.

strategies.name: Large-Base Conservative Jury
strategies.description: Ten proposers establish the base. Two challengers and three jurors must converge before changing it.

stages.instruction: Solve independently and report one exact answer.
Try to disprove the base plurality with a complete alternative mechanism.
Independently choose the answer that survives the challenge.

strategies.name: Distributed Four-Role Panel
strategies.description: Nine proposers are reviewed by critics, verifiers, and jurors with no single-agent authority.

stages.instruction: Solve independently and return one complete answer.
Falsify weak candidates and report the strongest survivor.
Reconstruct the strongest candidate exactly.
Choose one exact answer independently.

strategies.name: Proof-Auditor Supermajority
strategies.description: Ten proposers are checked by five proof auditors; three auditors must agree to change the base.

stages.instruction: Solve independently and provide a reproducible rule.
Accept an answer only if its mechanism replays every visible term.

strategies.name: Modular-Affine Task Force
strategies.description: A targeted solver and verification panel explicitly stress-tests modular affine mechanisms and wrap points.

stages.instruction: Actively test MODAFFINE first, including phase-specific affine maps and prime-modulus wrap points, but reject it when another allowed family fits better.
Reconstruct candidate modular maps and verify every wrap point and phase exactly; also test the strongest nonmodular rival.
Choose the fully replayed answer without assuming the case is modular.

strategies.name: Growing-Block Task Force
strategies.description: A targeted panel explicitly tests growing arithmetic blocks, boundaries, starts, and steps.

stages.instruction: Actively test GROWBLOCK first by locating block boundaries and solving polynomial starts and steps, but reject it when another family fully fits better.
Check alternate block boundaries and compare growing-block explanations with the strongest interleaved or lagged rival.
Choose the answer whose boundaries and arithmetic replay the full prefix.

strategies.name: Stress Arithmetic Review
strategies.description: Ten broad solvers are paired with five arithmetic and recurrence auditors using a judge-free weighted selector.

stages.instruction: Solve independently, preserving exact indexing and large-integer arithmetic.
Audit large values, signs, phase indexing, and recurrence replay. Return the corrected exact tuple.

strategies.name: Low-Consensus Verification Reserve
strategies.description: Ten solvers handle every case. Five verifiers are deployed only when the solver plurality has less than 70 percent support.

stages.instruction: Solve independently and report one complete answer.
For low-consensus cases only, reconstruct the strongest candidates and return the one that survives exact replay.

strategies.name: Mechanism Router and Specialist Bank
strategies.description: Three broad hypotheses seed a twelve-agent specialist bank spanning all mechanism families.

stages.instruction: Identify and solve the most plausible complete mechanism while noting serious family alternatives in the concise evidence fields.
Independently solve through the assigned specialty, using the routing evidence only as hypotheses to test rather than authority.

strategies.name: Dual Twelve-Solver Branches with Cross-Check
strategies.description: Two independent six-solver branches create a twelve-vote base; three challengers can change it only by two-agent agreement.

stages.instruction: Solve independently from scratch.
Solve independently from scratch without relying on the first branch.
Cross-check the two branches and report a replacement only when a complete mechanism defeats the base plurality.

strategies.name: Sequential Proof Repair
strategies.description: Nine proposals pass through criticism, verification, and one final proof integrator with a three-reviewer support gate.

stages.instruction: Solve independently and return one complete answer.
Identify concrete proposal failures and the strongest repaired answer.
Reconstruct the strongest survivor exactly.
Return the single answer that survives the complete proof audit.

strategies.name: Margin-Sensitive Jury
strategies.description: Ten solvers establish the base. A five-member jury is invoked only when the top two answers are within two votes.

stages.instruction: Solve independently and return one exact answer.
For close-vote cases only, independently resolve the leading candidates through complete replay.
